revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=志木市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[志木市]][[中宗岡]]1-4-36 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 50 |緯度秒 = 16.4 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 = 56.6 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[寛永]]3年（[[1626年]]）？ |本殿 = |別名 = 宗岡天神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[志木市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1626年]]（[[寛永]]3年）に創建されたと伝えられる。当地の旧[[名主]]で、つい最近まで[[境内]]の半分を所有していた木下氏が創建に関与していたものと推測される<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、538-539p</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]いろは橋停留所より徒歩2分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:志木市の歴史]] [[Category:志木市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=志木市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[志木市]][[中宗岡]]1-4-36 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 50 |緯度秒 = 16.4 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 = 56.6 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[寛永]]3年（[[1626年]]）？ |本殿 = |別名 = 宗岡天神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[志木市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1626年]]（[[寛永]]3年）に創建されたと伝えられる。当地の旧[[名主]]で、つい最近まで[[境内]]の半分を所有していた木下氏が創建に関与していたものと推測される<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、538-539p</ref>。
[[祭神]]は[[菅原道真]]である。道真が神として祀られる際、通常は「学問の神様」として崇敬されるが、当社では「五穀豊穣の神様」として崇敬されている。そういう経緯から、周辺には[[あさか野農業協同組合|JAあさか野]]志木支店や[[米穀店]]などが存在している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]いろは橋停留所より徒歩2分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年

== Tohoku Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tome)|ja|天神社 (登米市)}} - Located in {{ill|Tome, Miyagi|ja|登米市}}, Miyagi Prefecture.
== Kanto Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiroi)|ja|天神社 (白井市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiroi, Chiba|ja|白井市}}, Chiba Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Isumi) - Located in Isumi, Chiba Prefecture, in Misaki Town, Choja. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nishitokyo)|ja|天神社 (西東京市)}} - Located in {{ill|Nishitokyo|ja|西東京市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mitaka)|ja|天神社 (三鷹市)}} - Located in {{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Shiki)|ja|天神社 (志木市)}} - Located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kamisato)|ja|天神社 (上里町五明)}} - Located in Kamisato, Saitama. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Chuo)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市中央区)}} - Located in Saitama City, Chuo Ward. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Minamiku-Futonoya)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市見沼区風渡野)}} - Located in Futonoya, Minamiku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Saitama-Midoriku-Terayama)|ja|天神社 (さいたま市緑区寺山)}} - Located in Terayama, Midoriku, Saitama City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yashio-Ozone)|ja|天神社 (八潮市大曽根)}} - Located in Ozone, Yashio City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kuki-Kitahiroshima)|ja|天神社 (久喜市北広島)}} - Located in Kitahiroshima, Kuki City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Hanyu-Kamikawasaki)|ja|天神社 (羽生市上川崎)}} - Located in Kamikawasaki, Hanyu City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kazo-Hiranaga)|ja|天神社 (加須市平永)}} - Located in Hiranaga, Kazo City. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yokosuka)|ja|天神社 (横須賀市)}} ({{ill|Kurihama Tenjinja|ja|久里浜天神社}}) - Located in {{ill|Yokosuka|ja|横須賀市}}, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kurobe)|ja|天神社 (黒部市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kurobe, Toyama|ja|黒部市}}, Toyama Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Fujikawaguchiko-Oarashi)|ja|天神社 (富士河口湖町大嵐)}} - Located in Oarashi, {{ill|Fujikawaguchiko|ja|富士河口湖町}}, Yamanashi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Oarashi Tenjinja|ja|大嵐天神社}} * {{ill|Tenmantenjinja (Iida)|ja|天満天神社 (飯田市)}} - Located in Nakanomachi, {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, Nagano Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Fumon-in (Iida)|ja|普門院 (飯田市)}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nagoya)|ja|天神社 (名古屋市中区)}} - Located in {{ill|Naka-ku, Nagoya|ja|中区 (名古屋市)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Sakura Tenjinja|ja|桜天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Obu)|ja|天神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Nagasago, {{ill|Obu, Aichi|ja|大府市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Nagasago Tenjinja|ja|長草天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Toyokawa)|ja|天神社 (豊川市)}} - Located in Mikamicho, {{ill|Toyokawa, Aichi|ja|豊川市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Komaki)|ja|天神社 (小牧市)}} - Located in Shimazu, {{ill|Komaki|ja|小牧市}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ Tenmantenjinja * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kasukawa)|ja|天神社 (春日井市勝川町)}} - Located in Katsukawacho, {{ill|Kasugai|ja|春日井市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kiyosu)|ja|天神社 (清須市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kiyosu|ja|清須市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Mihama)|ja|天神社 (愛知県美浜町)}} - Located in {{ill|Mihama, Aichi|ja|美浜町 (愛知県)}}, Aichi Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kowa Tenjinja|ja|河和天神社}} * {{ill|Kitano Tenjinja (Konan)|ja|北野天神社 (江南市)}} - Located in Kitanocho, {{ill|Konan, Aichi|ja|江南市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Hokai Tenjinja|ja|宝海天神社}} - Located in Homi Town, {{ill|Tahara, Aichi|ja|田原市}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Yoshida Tenjinja|ja|吉田天神社}} - Located in Hirokoji, {{ill|Toyohashi, Aichi|ja|豊橋市}}, Aichi Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Osaka-Fukushima)|ja|天神社 (大阪市福島区)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima-ku, Osaka|ja|福島区}}, Osaka Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Osaka Kita-ku) - Located in Kokubunji, Kita-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Yodogawa Tenjinja|ja|淀川天神社}} * Tenjinja (Osaka Higashisumiyoshi-ku) - Located in Kuwazu, Higashisumiyoshi-ku, Osaka. ⇒ {{ill|Kuwazu Tenjinja|ja|桑津天神社}} * Tenjinja (Toyonaka) - Located in Kamishinden, {{ill|Toyonaka|ja|豊中市}}, Osaka Prefecture. ⇒ {{ill|Kamishinden Tenjinja|ja|上新田天神社}} * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kawachinagano)|ja|天神社 (河内長野市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kawachinagano|ja|河内長野市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kadoma)|ja|天神社 (門真市)}} - Located in Tsukidecho, {{ill|Kadoma|ja|門真市}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Kizugawa)|ja|天神社 (木津川市)}} - Located in Yamashiro, {{ill|Kizugawa|ja|木津川市}}, Kyoto Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Nara-Takabatake)|ja|天神社 (奈良市高畑町)}} - Located in Takabatakecho, {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Yamatotakada)|ja|天神社 (大和高田市)}} - Located in Miwacho, {{ill|Yamatotakada|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja (Sakurai) - Located in Saimiyama, {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture. * Tenjinja - Located in Kawahara 4-chome, Kusatsu, Shiga Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Akashi)|ja|天神社 (明石市)}} - Located in {{ill|Akashi|ja|明石市}}, Hyogo Prefecture.
== Shikoku Region == * {{ill|Tokushima Bizen Tenjinja|ja|徳島眉山天神社}} - Located in {{ill|Bizencho|ja|眉山町}}, {{ill|Tokushima|ja|徳島市}}, Tokushima Prefecture. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima)|ja|天神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in Kitamiya, Tokushima. * {{ill|Tenjinja (Tokushima-Iritacho)|ja|天神社 (徳島市入田町)}} - Located in Irita, Tokushima.
== Kyushu Region == * {{ill|Tenjinja (Itoshima)|ja|天神社 (糸島市)}} - Located in Kamiaru, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|ja|糸島市}}, Fukuoka Prefecture.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | Image = {{Image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | Location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Shrine Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | Annual Festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.

== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=志木市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[志木市]][[中宗岡]]1-4-36 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 50 |緯度秒 = 16.4 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 = 56.6 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[寛永]]3年（[[1626年]]）？ |本殿 = |別名 = 宗岡天神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[志木市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1626年]]（[[寛永]]3年）に創建されたと伝えられる。当地の旧[[名主]]で、つい最近まで[[境内]]の半分を所有していた木下氏が創建に関与していたものと推測される<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、538-539p</ref>。
[[祭神]]は[[菅原道真]]である。道真が神として祀られる際、通常は「学問の神様」として崇敬されるが、当社では「五穀豊穣の神様」として崇敬されている。そういう経緯から、周辺には[[あさか野農業協同組合|JAあさか野]]志木支店や[[米穀店]]などが存在している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
境内の「水神宮」の碑文は[[神山雲眠]]の筆によるものである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.shiki.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/5806.pdf|title=志木市文化財・歴史ガイド|date=|publisher=志木市|accessdate=2025-3-2}}</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]いろは橋停留所より徒歩2分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:志木市の歴史]] [[Category:志木市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "菅原道真" to "Sugawara no Michizane"
revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=draft}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=draft}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=draft}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=draft}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=draft}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=draft}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

{{Shinto Shrine | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=draft}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|ja|志木市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県|12=draft}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=draft}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=draft}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 天神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=志木市}} |所在地 = [[埼玉県]][[志木市]][[中宗岡]]1-4-36 |緯度度 = 35 |緯度分 = 50 |緯度秒 = 16.4 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 = 56.6 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = 村社 |創建 = [[寛永]]3年（[[1626年]]）？ |本殿 = |別名 = 宗岡天神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天神社'''（てんじんしゃ）は、[[埼玉県]][[志木市]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1626年]]（[[寛永]]3年）に創建されたと伝えられる。当地の旧[[名主]]で、つい最近まで[[境内]]の半分を所有していた木下氏が創建に関与していたものと推測される<ref name="神社">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年、538-539p</ref>。
[[祭神]]は[[菅原道真]]である。道真が神として祀られる際、通常は「学問の神様」として崇敬されるが、当社では「五穀豊穣の神様」として崇敬されている。そういう経緯から、周辺には[[あさか野農業協同組合|JAあさか野]]志木支店や[[米穀店]]などが存在している<ref name="神社"></ref>。
境内の「水神宮」の碑文は[[神山雲眠]]の筆によるものである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.shiki.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/5806.pdf|title=志木市文化財・歴史ガイド|date=|publisher=志木市|accessdate=2025-3-2}}</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == *[[路線バス]]いろは橋停留所より徒歩2分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 北足立・児玉・南埼玉』埼玉県神社庁、1998年
{{Pref-stub|pref=埼玉県}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんしんしや}} [[Category:埼玉県の神社]] [[Category:志木市の歴史]] [[Category:志木市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の旧村社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|qq=|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|qq=|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|qq=|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|qq=|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|qq=|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|qq=|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>

While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>

The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.
