'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shiki, Saitama|Shiki, Saitama]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]].
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q128866564]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:53, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>

{{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=JA Asakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama|1=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202|1=Shiki, Saitama}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama|1=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=Q11257146|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama|1=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=Q11257146|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.

It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>

While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=Q11257146|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天神社 (志木市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| | Name = Tenjinsha | image = {{image requested|cat=Shiki, Saitama}} | location = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama|1=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Nakasouka|ja|中宗岡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakasouka|1=Nakamuneoka, Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21653929}} 1-4-36 | Latitude = 35°50'16.4" N | Longitude = 139°34'56.6" E | Map = | Enshrined deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | type = Village Shrine (村社) | Founded = {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 3 (1626)? | Main Hall = | Other Names = Sōka Tenjinsha (宗岡天神社) | Pilgrimage Sites = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Shiki|WD=Q877202|1=Shiki, Saitama}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.837889|N|139.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama|1=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=Q11257146|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tenjinsha''' (天神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shiki, Saitama|en|Shiki, Saitama|WD=Q877202|lt=Shiki, Saitama|1=Shiki, Saitama}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.
== History == The shrine is believed to have been founded in 1626 (Kan'ei 3). It is speculated that the {{ill|Kinoshita family|ja|木下氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoshita family|1=Kinoshita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11518060}}, former village heads who owned half of the shrine grounds until recently, were involved in its establishment.<ref name="Shrine">Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998, pp. 538–539.</ref>
The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}. While Michizane is typically revered as the "god of learning," at this shrine he is worshiped as the "god of abundant harvests." Reflecting this agricultural connection, the surrounding area includes facilities like the {{ill|JA Asakano|ja|あさか野農業協同組合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JA Asakano|1=Q11257146|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257146}} Shiki Branch and rice shops.<ref name="Shrine"></ref>
== Access == * A 2-minute walk from the Irohabashi bus stop on the local bus route.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Saitama Prefecture Shrine Research Group (Ed.), *Shrines of Saitama: Kita-Adachi, Kodama, Minami-Saitama*, Saitama Prefecture Shrine Association, 1998.
